one_liner: Power wheelchair for outdoor use — complete file, in-home standard genuinely unmet, denial supported
clinical_summary: 72M with mild COPD. Face-to-face mobility exam documents independent ambulation throughout the home with a rollator walker, including safe transfers, toileting, and kitchen access. Home assessment confirms single-level home with no barriers to walker use. Stated goal on the exam: 'wants the power chair for longer outings — the senior center and the park are too far to walk.'

draft_decision.rationale: In-home mobility-related activities of daily living are adequately and safely accomplished with the current rollator walker per the face-to-face exam and home assessment.
draft_decision.specific_reason: Coverage requires that a mobility limitation significantly impair activities of daily living in the home that cannot be resolved with a cane, walker, or manual chair. Your exam shows your walker safely meets your needs at home; equipment primarily for use outside the home does not meet the coverage standard.
